title: Sinners Had 16 Oscar Nominations: Here's How It Only Won 4
text: Sinners received a record 16 Oscar nominations at the 98th Academy Awards, the most of any film in history. Despite this unprecedented number, the film won only 4 awards: Best Actor (Michael B. Jordan), Best Original Screenplay (Ryan Coogler), Best Original Score (Ludwig Göransson), and Best Cinematography (Autumn Durald Arkapaw). Michael B. Jordan's win was particularly significant as it marked his first Oscar nomination and win, and made him the first performer in Oscar history to win Best Actor for a dual role in the same film. The film did not win Best Picture, which went to Paul Thomas Anderson's One Battle After Another. This significant gap between nominations and wins is not unusual in Oscar history, where films with high nomination counts often compete in numerous categories and may split votes or face strong competition. The nominations reflected the film's technical achievements, strong performances, and broad appeal across the Academy. The four wins nonetheless represented major achievements for the film and its creators, including the historic victories for cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw becoming the first woman to win in that category.
